learning_outcomes.description: Critically evaluate contemporary scientific evidence and research methodologies relevant to organisational psychology research and practice.

Design and apply appropriate research methodologies — including experimental, quasi-experimental, survey, qualitative, and quantitative approaches — to investigate problems within organisational settings.
Select and justify measurement strategies and analytic approaches appropriate to applied organisational research questions, taking into account sampling, design, and contextual considerations.
Demonstrate effective collaboration and teamwork skills when addressing complex organisational problems.

Communicate research evidence and methodological approaches effectively in oral formats to relevant stakeholder groups, applying ethical reasoning and contextually appropriate language in applied organisational research contexts.

assessments.assessment_title: CBOP Research Seminar Review and Critique
assessments.task_description: Attend (or review a recording) of a CBOP Research Seminar - identify the key aims and scope of the research and then evaluate the merits - theoretically, methodologically, and practically.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date:
8/10/2026 10:00 am

Students will present during the scheduled class time.
assessments.task_description: In groups of 5-6 deliver an oral presentation in the form of a research proposal on the organisational problem outlined in the case study provided (worth 30 marks). You will imagine that the lecturer and other students are the stakeholders who will decide whether or not to fund your proposed research project. Therefore, you are required to communicate the background research, unique focus, and proposed methodology of your project in a manner appropriate for your stakeholder group (e.g., community group members, school principal and teachers, company director, or a senior management team, etc., depending on the case study assigned). You will also be required to complete peer assessment on your other group members after the presentations (worth 10 marks).
assessments.submission_guidelines: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description: Via a Blackboard Ultra, students will gain access to a case study involving applied research. Students will have a ~ two week period during which they can access the case study and then submit their responses by the due date.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
